articleSummary: The article presents a tutorial-style guide for creating bash scripts to automate Linux server benchmarking, focusing on CPU and HDD performance using tools like `sysbench`, `inxi`, and `iotop`. The core thesis emphasizes simplifying repetitive benchmarking tasks by encapsulating complex command-line arguments into reusable shell functions, with a Debian/Ubuntu-centric setup. Key features include automated dependency installation, dynamic test configuration (e.g., thread counts, block sizes), and modular functions for sequential/random I/O and CPU stress tests. The intended audience is system administrators or DevOps engineers seeking streamlined performance evaluation workflows. The tone is practical and instructional, framed as a "library" of scripts to reduce manual effort while ensuring consistent, reproducible benchmarking.
